channel.title: KillBait RSS Feed
channel.description: KillBait - News highlights delivered clearly and responsibly—no clickbait, no sensationalism

item.title: Global leaders are engaging more with China, but Beijing’s diplomatic influence still faces major limits
item.description: The article examines China’s growing diplomatic visibility following a series of high-profile visits to Beijing by world leaders including Vladimir Putin, Donald Trump, Mark Carney and leaders from se...

item.title: Inmate claims relationship with former prison officer Linda De Sousa Abreu began through inappropriate favours at HMP Wandsworth
item.description: Linton Weirich, a convicted burglar who was filmed engaging in sexual activity with prison officer Linda De Sousa Abreu inside HMP Wandsworth in June 2024, has spoken publicly about the incident and t...

item.title: Ilorin Emirate Unveils Detailed Plans for 2026 Grand Durbar Celebration
item.description: The Ilorin Emirate Durbar Committee has come out with plenty plans for the 2026 Grand Durbar wey go hold on Thursday, May 28, 2026 at Kwara Baseball Park for Adewole, Ilorin. According to the committe...

item.title: Polly Toynbee argues that Labour’s renewal depends on leadership change, a new manifesto, and electoral reform
item.description: In this opinion piece, Polly Toynbee argues that the future of the Labour Party and wider British politics could be reshaped by a potential leadership shift towards Andy Burnham, combined with a bold ...

item.title: CNET Editor Questions Design and Reliability of Trump Mobile Smartphone During CNN Interview
item.description: A CNN segment featuring CNET Managing Editor Patrick Holland drew attention to President Donald Trump's new business venture, Trump Mobile, and its gold-colored smartphone known as the T1. During the ...

item.title: A Look Back at Teri Garr’s Rise to Fame Through 1970s Portraits and Film Roles
item.description: The article from Vintage Everyday highlights the career and public image of American actress, comedian, and dancer Teri Garr during the 1970s, accompanied by a collection of 20 vintage portraits. Born...

item.title: Senator Emmanuel Bwacha confirmed as PDP governorship candidate for Taraba State
item.description: Senator Emmanuel Bwacha, former Deputy Senate Minority Leader, has been confirmed as the sole governorship candidate of the Peoples Democratic Party (PDP) for the 2027 general election in Taraba State...

item.title: Australian woman subject to exclusion order remains in Syria as other Islamic State-linked women and children return
item.description: Nineteen Australian women and children linked to Islamic State departed Syria for Australia on Monday night, while one woman, Hodan Abby, and her nine-year-old daughter were left behind after the fede...

item.title: Doctor explains how diet and gut health may influence anxiety and mental wellbeing
item.description: This article explores the growing scientific understanding of the connection between nutrition, gut health, and mental wellbeing, particularly anxiety. According to Dr. Kunal Sood, an anaesthesiologis...

item.title: Pope Leo XIV Calls for Ethical Safeguards and Human Dignity in the Age of Artificial Intelligence
item.description: In a significant new encyclical titled 'Magnifica Humanitas', Pope Leo XIV has urged global leaders, corporate executives, and individuals to protect humanity from the disruptive impacts of artificial...

item.title: NYT analysis finds frequent praise and flattery in Trump cabinet meetings
item.description: A New York Times report examined the tone and content of Trump administration cabinet meetings, concluding that a significant portion of the discussion is devoted to praising President Donald Trump. A...

item.title: The Story of Y Bwthyn Bach, Queen Elizabeth II’s Childhood Cottage in Windsor Great Park
item.description: Y Bwthyn Bach, meaning “The Little House” in Welsh, was a miniature cottage gifted to Princess Elizabeth, the future Queen Elizabeth II, for her sixth birthday in 1932. The playhouse was presented by ...

item.title: Health experts raise concerns over heat, infectious diseases and food safety ahead of FIFA World Cup kickoff
item.description: As the FIFA World Cup scheduled to hold across the United States, Mexico, and Canada from June 11 to July 19 draws near, health experts and government officials are expressing concerns about possible ...

item.title: Arsenal win the 2025-26 Premier League title after dramatic season filled with managerial changes and late goals
item.description: The article reviews the fictional 2025-26 Premier League season month by month through a collection of memorable photographs and key moments. Liverpool began the campaign strongly despite the emotiona...

item.title: JD Vance Invites Republican Attorneys General Earlier Than Democrats for Fraud Task Force Meeting
item.description: Vice President JD Vance has been tasked by President Donald Trump with leading the administration's efforts to combat fraud. As part of this initiative, a White House meeting was scheduled for Tuesday...

item.title: The Knicks’ Winning Streak and the Danhausen ‘Curse’ Narrative Explained
item.description: The article explores a humorous and speculative sports storyline linking the New York Knicks’ recent winning streak to professional wrestler Danhausen and his self-described “curse.” The narrative beg...

item.title: Passengers evacuated from UK train after air conditioning failure during record-breaking heatwave
item.description: Passengers travelling on a Chiltern Railways service from London Marylebone to Stourbridge Junction were forced to disembark after the train’s air conditioning system failed during an exceptional heat...

item.title: Vintage Photographs Capture Daily Life at Chicago’s Riverview Amusement Park in 1962
item.description: The article highlights a collection of vintage photographs taken by photographer Laird Scott during the summer of 1962 at Chicago’s famous Riverview Amusement Park. Once considered one of the largest ...

item.title: Grigori Rasputin: Historical Portraits and His Role as Advisor to the Russian Imperial Family
item.description: Grigori Yefimovich Rasputin was a Russian mystic and faith healer born in 1869 in a poor Siberian peasant family. Known by nicknames such as the 'Mad Monk' or 'Holy Devil,' he rose to prominence as a ...

item.title: Leo Woodall and Dustin Hoffman shine in understated crime drama Tuner
item.description: The Guardian’s review of Tuner praises the chemistry between Leo Woodall and Dustin Hoffman in a restrained crime drama that blends elements of romance, comedy and thriller storytelling. Woodall plays...
